seoTitle: Broughton Village vs Brownlow Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Broughton Village and Brownlow Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Broughton Village vs Brownlow Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Broughton Village and Brownlow Hill.
heading: Broughton Village vs Brownlow Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Broughton Village, New South Wales and Brownlow Hill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Broughton Village has a lower overall climate risk score (47/100) compared to Brownlow Hill (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Broughton Village or Brownlow Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Broughton Village has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Brownlow Hill scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Broughton Village scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Brownlow Hill scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Broughton Village or Brownlow Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Broughton Village has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
